title: America's Diet Advice Is Written by Industry
description: The committee that writes America's official dietary guidelines — the document shaping school lunches, food assistance programs, and clinical nutrition advice — had 19 of 20 members with documented financial ties to food and pharmaceutical companies in its most recent iteration. This is not a new problem, and it is not an accident. This piece traces the specific mechanisms by which industry shapes official nutrition advice, from the 1960s sugar industry playbook to the institutional capture of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics today.
